name: authoring
context: Write, test, document, and publish reusable Terraform modules following HashiCorp best practices
info: This skill covers the full lifecycle of authoring a Terraform module: standard directory structure, input variable design with validation, output design for downstream consumers, documentation generation with terraform-docs, testing with terratest and the built-in terraform test framework (1.6+), and versioning with git tags. A well-authored module has a single responsibility, minimal required variables with sane defaults, comprehensive outputs, and automated tests. See [[terraform/modules]] for module fundamentals and [[terraform/modules/registry]] for publishing to the Terraform Registry.
triggers: how to write a terraform module
body:
# Authoring Terraform Modules

A well-authored Terraform module is a reusable building block for infrastructure. It follows a standard directory structure, validates its inputs, exposes useful outputs, includes documentation, and has automated tests.

## Standard Module Structure

```
modules/vpc/
|-- main.tf          # Resources
|-- variables.tf     # Input variables
|-- outputs.tf       # Output values
|-- versions.tf      # Provider requirements
|-- locals.tf        # Local values and computed expressions
|-- README.md        # Documentation (auto-generated with terraform-docs)
|-- examples/
|   |-- simple/
|   |   |-- main.tf
|   |-- complete/
|       |-- main.tf
|-- test/
    |-- vpc_test.go  # Terratest tests
```

Each file has a clear purpose:

- **main.tf** -- resource definitions, data sources, and module calls
- **variables.tf** -- all input variable declarations with descriptions and defaults
- **outputs.tf** -- all output value declarations with descriptions
- **versions.tf** -- `terraform` block with `required_version` and `required_providers`
- **locals.tf** -- computed values, name prefixes, tag merging

## versions.tf

```hcl
terraform {
  required_version = ">= 1.5.0"

  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = ">= 5.0"
    }
  }
}
```

## Input Variable Design

Design variables with the consumer in mind. Minimize required inputs; provide sane defaults:

```hcl
variable "name" {
  description = "Name prefix for all resources"
  type        = string

  validation {
    condition     = length(var.name) > 0 && length(var.name) <= 32
    error_message = "Name must be 1-32 characters."
  }
}

variable "cidr_block" {
  description = "CIDR block for the VPC"
  type        = string
  default     = "10.0.0.0/16"

  validation {
    condition     = can(cidrhost(var.cidr_block, 0))
    error_message = "Must be a valid CIDR block."
  }
}

variable "environment" {
  description = "Deployment environment"
  type        = string
  default     = "dev"

  validation {
    condition     = contains(["dev", "staging", "production"], var.environment)
    error_message = "Environment must be dev, staging, or production."
  }
}

variable "tags" {
  description = "Additional tags to apply to all resources"
  type        = map(string)
  default     = {}
}
```

## Output Design

Expose enough for downstream modules and compositions. Include the resource ID, ARN, and any connection details:

```hcl
output "vpc_id" {
  description = "The ID of the VPC"
  value       = aws_vpc.this.id
}

output "vpc_arn" {
  description = "The ARN of the VPC"
  value       = aws_vpc.this.arn
}

output "private_subnet_ids" {
  description = "List of private subnet IDs"
  value       = aws_subnet.private[*].id
}

output "public_subnet_ids" {
  description = "List of public subnet IDs"
  value       = aws_subnet.public[*].id
}

output "database_subnet_ids" {
  description = "List of database subnet IDs"
  value       = aws_subnet.database[*].id
}

output "nat_gateway_ips" {
  description = "List of NAT Gateway public IPs"
  value       = aws_eip.nat[*].public_ip
}
```

## Testing with terraform test (1.6+)

Terraform 1.6 introduced a built-in test framework using `.tftest.hcl` files:

```hcl
# tests/vpc.tftest.hcl
run "creates_vpc" {
  command = plan

  variables {
    name       = "test-vpc"
    cidr_block = "10.0.0.0/16"
  }

  assert {
    condition     = aws_vpc.this.cidr_block == "10.0.0.0/16"
    error_message = "VPC CIDR block does not match expected value"
  }
}

run "validates_cidr" {
  command = plan

  variables {
    name       = "test"
    cidr_block = "not-a-cidr"
  }

  expect_failures = [var.cidr_block]
}
```

Run with `terraform test`.

## Testing with Terratest (Go)

For integration tests that create and destroy real infrastructure:

```go
package test

import (
    "testing"
    "github.com/gruntwork-io/terratest/modules/terraform"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
)

func TestVpcModule(t *testing.T) {
    t.Parallel()

    terraformOptions := terraform.WithDefaultRetryableErrors(t, &terraform.Options{
        TerraformDir: "../examples/complete",
        Vars: map[string]interface{}{
            "name":       "test-vpc",
            "cidr_block": "10.0.0.0/16",
        },
    })

    defer terraform.Destroy(t, terraformOptions)
    terraform.InitAndApply(t, terraformOptions)

    vpcId := terraform.Output(t, terraformOptions, "vpc_id")
    assert.Regexp(t, `^vpc-`, vpcId)

    privateSubnetIds := terraform.OutputList(t, terraformOptions, "private_subnet_ids")
    assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, len(privateSubnetIds), 2)
}
```

## Documentation with terraform-docs

Auto-generate README from variable and output declarations:

```bash
# Install
brew install terraform-docs

# Generate markdown table format
terraform-docs markdown table --output-file README.md ./modules/vpc

# Use a config file for consistent formatting
cat > .terraform-docs.yml << 'CONF'
formatter: markdown table
output:
  file: README.md
  mode: inject
sort:
  enabled: true
  by: required
CONF

terraform-docs -c .terraform-docs.yml ./modules/vpc
```

## Versioning Strategy

Use git tags with semantic versioning:

```bash
# Tag a release
git tag -a v1.0.0 -m "Initial release of VPC module"
git push origin v1.0.0

# Consumers pin to the tag
# source = "git::https://github.com/org/terraform-aws-vpc.git?ref=v1.0.0"
```

Follow semver: bump **major** for breaking changes to variables or outputs, **minor** for new features (new optional variables, new outputs), **patch** for bug fixes.

sections.title: Standard module structure
sections.body: A Terraform module follows a standard file layout: `main.tf` for resources, `variables.tf` for inputs, `outputs.tf` for outputs, `versions.tf` for provider requirements, and `locals.tf` for computed values. Include an `examples/` directory with at least a `simple/` and `complete/` example. Include a `test/` directory for automated tests. This structure is expected by the Terraform Registry and community tooling like terraform-docs.

sections.title: Module design best practices
sections.body: Follow single responsibility: one module does one thing well (VPC, EKS cluster, RDS instance). Minimize required variables -- provide sane defaults for everything that has a reasonable default. Use variable validation blocks to catch errors at plan time. Expose comprehensive outputs (IDs, ARNs, endpoints, connection strings) so downstream modules can consume without data source lookups. Avoid hardcoding provider configurations -- let the root module configure providers. Use `locals` for computed values and tag merging. Never use `terraform_remote_state` inside a module -- accept values as variables instead.

sections.title: Testing Terraform modules
sections.body: Two primary approaches: (1) `terraform test` (built-in, Terraform 1.6+) uses `.tftest.hcl` files for plan-level and apply-level assertions without external tools. (2) Terratest (Go) creates real infrastructure, runs assertions against it, and destroys it. Terratest is more powerful but slower and requires Go and cloud credentials. Use `terraform test` for fast validation of variable constraints and plan-time checks. Use Terratest for integration tests that verify real resource creation. Both can run in CI pipelines. Also consider `terraform validate` for syntax checks and `tflint` for linting.

sections.title: Documentation with terraform-docs
sections.body: Use `terraform-docs` to auto-generate README.md from variable and output declarations. Supports markdown table, markdown document, and other formats. Configure via `.terraform-docs.yml` for consistent output. Run in CI to ensure docs stay in sync with code. The Terraform Registry renders README.md as the module's documentation page. Every variable and output should have a `description` attribute -- terraform-docs uses these to generate the docs. Include usage examples in the README header.

scripts.name: Scaffold Terraform Module
scripts.description: Create a new Terraform module directory with standard file structure, examples, and test stubs
scripts.inline:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Scaffold a new Terraform module with standard structure
# Usage: MODULE_NAME=vpc PROVIDER=aws bash scaffold-module.sh

MODULE_NAME="${MODULE_NAME:?MODULE_NAME is required (e.g. vpc, eks, rds)}"
PROVIDER="${PROVIDER:-aws}"
MODULE_DIR="${MODULE_DIR:-./modules/${MODULE_NAME}}"

echo "Scaffolding Terraform module: ${MODULE_NAME} (provider: ${PROVIDER})"
echo "Directory: ${MODULE_DIR}"

mkdir -p "${MODULE_DIR}/examples/simple"
mkdir -p "${MODULE_DIR}/examples/complete"
mkdir -p "${MODULE_DIR}/test"

# main.tf
cat > "${MODULE_DIR}/main.tf" << 'HCL'
# -----------------------------------------------
# Main resources for this module
# -----------------------------------------------

# TODO: Add your resources here
HCL

# variables.tf
cat > "${MODULE_DIR}/variables.tf" << HCL
variable "name" {
  description = "Name prefix for all resources created by this module"
  type        = string

  validation {
    condition     = length(var.name) > 0 && length(var.name) <= 64
    error_message = "Name must be between 1 and 64 characters."
  }
}

variable "environment" {
  description = "Deployment environment (dev, staging, production)"
  type        = string
  default     = "dev"

  validation {
    condition     = contains(["dev", "staging", "production"], var.environment)
    error_message = "Environment must be dev, staging, or production."
  }
}

variable "tags" {
  description = "Additional tags to apply to all resources"
  type        = map(string)
  default     = {}
}
HCL

# outputs.tf
cat > "${MODULE_DIR}/outputs.tf" << 'HCL'
# -----------------------------------------------
# Outputs
# -----------------------------------------------

# TODO: Add outputs for downstream consumers
# output "id" {
#   description = "The ID of the primary resource"
#   value       = aws_xxx.this.id
# }
HCL

# versions.tf
cat > "${MODULE_DIR}/versions.tf" << HCL
terraform {
  required_version = ">= 1.5.0"

  required_providers {
    ${PROVIDER} = {
      source  = "hashicorp/${PROVIDER}"
      version = ">= 5.0"
    }
  }
}
HCL

# locals.tf
cat > "${MODULE_DIR}/locals.tf" << HCL
locals {
  common_tags = merge(
    {
      Module      = "${MODULE_NAME}"
      Environment = var.environment
      ManagedBy   = "terraform"
    },
    var.tags,
  )
}
HCL

# examples/simple/main.tf
cat > "${MODULE_DIR}/examples/simple/main.tf" << HCL
module "${MODULE_NAME}" {
  source = "../../"

  name        = "example"
  environment = "dev"
}
HCL

# examples/complete/main.tf
cat > "${MODULE_DIR}/examples/complete/main.tf" << HCL
module "${MODULE_NAME}" {
  source = "../../"

  name        = "example-complete"
  environment = "production"

  tags = {
    Project = "example"
    Owner   = "team"
  }
}

output "${MODULE_NAME}_id" {
  value = module.${MODULE_NAME}.id
}
HCL

# test/module_test.go
cat > "${MODULE_DIR}/test/${MODULE_NAME}_test.go" << GOTEST
package test

import (
    "testing"
    "github.com/gruntwork-io/terratest/modules/terraform"
)

func Test${MODULE_NAME^}Module(t *testing.T) {
    t.Parallel()

    terraformOptions := terraform.WithDefaultRetryableErrors(t, &terraform.Options{
        TerraformDir: "../examples/simple",
        Vars: map[string]interface{}{
            "name": "test",
        },
    })

    defer terraform.Destroy(t, terraformOptions)
    terraform.InitAndApply(t, terraformOptions)

    // TODO: Add assertions
}
GOTEST

# tests/basic.tftest.hcl (terraform test)
mkdir -p "${MODULE_DIR}/tests"
cat > "${MODULE_DIR}/tests/basic.tftest.hcl" << 'HCL'
run "plan_basic" {
  command = plan

  variables {
    name        = "test"
    environment = "dev"
  }
}

run "invalid_environment" {
  command = plan

  variables {
    name        = "test"
    environment = "invalid"
  }

  expect_failures = [var.environment]
}
HCL

# .terraform-docs.yml
cat > "${MODULE_DIR}/.terraform-docs.yml" << 'YAML'
formatter: markdown table
output:
  file: README.md
  mode: inject
sort:
  enabled: true
  by: required
settings:
  anchor: true
  default: true
  required: true
  type: true
YAML

# README.md stub
cat > "${MODULE_DIR}/README.md" << MD
# ${MODULE_NAME} module

Terraform module for managing ${MODULE_NAME} resources.

## Usage

\`\`\`hcl
module "${MODULE_NAME}" {
  source = "path/to/modules/${MODULE_NAME}"

  name        = "my-${MODULE_NAME}"
  environment = "production"
}
\`\`\`

<!-- BEGIN_TF_DOCS -->
<!-- END_TF_DOCS -->
MD

echo ""
echo "Module scaffolded at ${MODULE_DIR}/"
echo "Files created:"
find "${MODULE_DIR}" -type f | sort | sed 's|^|  |'
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo "  1. Add resources to ${MODULE_DIR}/main.tf"
echo "  2. Define variables in ${MODULE_DIR}/variables.tf"
echo "  3. Expose outputs in ${MODULE_DIR}/outputs.tf"
echo "  4. Run: terraform-docs -c ${MODULE_DIR}/.terraform-docs.yml ${MODULE_DIR}"
echo "  5. Run: cd ${MODULE_DIR} && terraform test"

MODULE_NAME.description: Name of the module to scaffold (e.g. vpc, eks, rds)

PROVIDER.description: Terraform provider name (e.g. aws, azurerm, google)

moduleName.description: Name of the module to scaffold (e.g. vpc, eks, rds)

moduleDir.description: Target directory path for the module
Path to the scaffolded module directory
